changes_summary: Tightened the audit wrapper design (Step 8) to KEEP the tool body inside the existing transaction — only post-commit network IO is queued — preserving the Sprint 1a rollback test (closes correctness-1, FLAG-009, callers). Added a durable `request_body` JSONB column to `external_requests` via a new migration (Step 4); the loop now persists the full Anthropic messages/tools payload at `record_pending` time so the reconciler can replay with the stored idempotency key (closes FLAG-007, correctness-2). Renamed the resident-mode hook from `on_pre_finalize` to `mid_turn_message_check` and made `run_turn` invoke it both at final-text time AND before each explicit `send_message` tool call, so the resident `send_message` cannot bypass the spec's same-turn check (closes FLAG-011, scope). Added a `Ledger` instance to `DiscordTransport` and required ingestion-side voice/Storage/Groq calls to record pending `external_requests` rows keyed off `discord_message_id`; reconciler covers them like any other pending row (closes FLAG-012, issue_hints). Extended `Blob` Protocol with `exists()` and `PushTransport` Protocol with `fetch_recent_messages()` since the reconciler calls both (closes all_locations). Dropped reliance on `request_summary` for replay material; kept it for diagnostic shape only.

flags_addressed.reason: Step 4 adds a `request_body` JSONB column to `external_requests`; Step 5 has the loop persist the full canonical Anthropic body (`model`, `messages`, `tools`, `max_tokens`) at `record_pending` time; Step 16 reconstructs the Anthropic replay from this column rather than from the small `request_summary`. Anthropic dedupe via the stored `idempotency_key` works against the durable body.

Step 8 keeps the tool body INSIDE the existing `store.transaction()` exactly as in Sprint 1a. Only post-commit network callables are queued. The existing rollback test at `tests/test_tool_kit.py:27` is preserved verbatim and listed as a test that must keep passing.

Same as FLAG-007: durable `request_body` column gives the reconciler the full message/tool body needed to reissue with the stored idempotency key. Plan also adds a dedicated `tests/test_anthropic_replay.py` that asserts the stub Anthropic receives the original body.
Audit wrapper redesigned to keep mutation+tool_calls atomic in the same transaction as before. Network IO is the only thing moved post-commit. Existing rollback semantics are preserved.

Step 6.2 makes `run_turn` run the mid-turn check at TWO checkpoints: (a) before final-text auto-send, and (b) BEFORE each explicit `send_message` tool invocation. An explicit model-issued `send_message` cannot post to Discord while unprocessed mid-turn messages exist.

Step 13.2 makes `DiscordTransport` take a `Ledger` and inserts pending `external_requests` rows for every ingestion-side IO call (Storage upload, Groq transcription) keyed off `discord_message_id` (new `ingest_message_id` branch in `derive_idempotency_key`). Step 13.4 walks through the per-attachment ledger sequence. Recovery scan (Step 16) reconciles them like any other pending row.

Same as FLAG-012: ingestion-side IO is now ledgered. Restart safety covers voice transcription, Storage upload, and image upload; the reconciler's per-provider dispatch already handles `groq` and `supabase_storage` rows.
Same as FLAG-011: the mid-turn check now gates explicit `send_message` tool calls in addition to the final-text path. The 'still has uncovered path' from iteration 2 is closed.

Step 9.2 adds `Blob.exists(ref) -> bool` to the `Blob` Protocol. Step 9.3 adds `fetch_recent_messages(channel_id, since, until) -> list[JSONDict]` to the new `PushTransport` Protocol. Both methods are now part of the published interfaces the reconciler calls.

Same as correctness-1 / FLAG-009: tool body still runs inside the audit transaction, so the `ToolRegistry.invoke` caller contract is unchanged. Existing tools that mutate the store directly (e.g. `send_message` invocation path) keep working without refactoring. The new external-queue mechanism is purely additive: tools that don't queue anything see no behavioral change.

questions: Confirm the Supabase service-role JWT pasted in the idea block has been (or will be) rotated. The plan does not commit it; all adapters read from `SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY` env. Please paste future secrets in env-var form, not in plan inputs.

success_criteria.criterion: DM from a whitelisted user triggers a `run_turn` invocation and a Discord reply within 30s in a mocked end-to-end test (FakeDiscordTransport + FakeModel + SQLiteStore)
DM from a non-whitelisted user produces no Discord reply and writes a `system_logs` row at level=info, category=application, event_type=whitelist_rejected
Every inbound Discord message persists with a unique `discord_message_id` before any LLM call, and a duplicate `discord_message_id` insert is rejected by the DB unique constraint
The unmodified `tests/store_contract.py` from Sprint 1a passes against both `SQLiteStore` and `SupabaseStore`

The new `tests/store_contract_v1b.py` (covering find_abandoned_turns, find_pending_external_requests, mark_orphaned, find_unprocessed_messages, load_messages, image CRUD, idempotency-key uniqueness, and `request_body` round-trip) passes against both stores
5 inbound messages within 8s coalesce into a single turn whose `triggered_by_message_ids` has 5 entries
Killing the runner mid-turn and restarting causes the prior turn to be marked `abandoned` in `bot_turns` and its trigger messages to be re-processed under a fresh `turn_id`

Mocked Groq voice-message pipeline produces a `messages` row with `was_voice_message=true`, `content=<transcription>`, `audio_storage_url` populated, AND two `external_requests` rows (provider=supabase_storage and provider=groq) both pending→confirmed

Mocked Discord image-attachment pipeline downloads to (mock) Supabase Storage, creates an `images` row with `source='user_uploaded'` and an auto-assigned `reference_key` matching `^img_user_upload_\d+$`, AND records a `supabase_storage` external_requests row pending→confirmed
`view_image` result with media_type + base64 bytes is rendered into an Anthropic vision content block on the next model call

Resident `send_message` posts via the injected push transport, fills `messages.discord_message_id` with the returned Discord id AFTER the audit transaction commits, and transitions an `external_requests` row pending→confirmed; assertion confirms `store.transaction_depth == 0` when the queued callable runs

Existing tool atomicity preserved: a tool that mutates the store and then raises rolls back the mutation AND does not record a `tool_calls` row — the Sprint 1a test at `tests/test_tool_kit.py:27` passes UNCHANGED

Status message lifecycle: `on_turn_start` posts initial status; each tool call → status edit (1s debounce); `set_activity` updates `bot_turns.current_activity` and the next edit reflects 'Currently:'; turn complete → final edit text starts with '✅ Done.' and contains the tool_call count
Status throttling: 20 tool calls within 2s produce at most ~4 Discord edit calls (1s debounce + final), not 20

Mid-turn message in the SAME turn: arrives during tool phase → persisted; status gets '📥 Received'; `mid_turn_message_check` causes `run_turn` to re-prompt the model with the spec block AND widen the existing turn's `triggered_by_message_ids` (NOT a follow-up turn)

Explicit-send_message gating: when the model issues a `send_message` tool call while unprocessed mid-turn messages exist, the loop runs the mid-turn check FIRST and re-prompts before any Discord post fires

Anthropic idempotency-key replay: the reconciler reissues `model.complete_turn` with the stored `idempotency_key` and the FULL `messages`/`tools`/`model` body taken from `external_requests.request_body`. Verified via `tests/test_anthropic_replay.py`

External-request reconciliation passes for Anthropic (replay via stored body+key), Discord (post-hoc message lookup via `transport.fetch_recent_messages` → confirmed; not found → orphaned), Groq (deterministic re-issue), Supabase Storage (HEAD via `Blob.exists` → confirmed; missing → re-issue)

Tool external-IO ordering: ledger row is `pending` before commit; DB transaction commits before any queued external callable runs; ledger row is `confirmed` after the callable returns. Verified via `tests/test_tool_kit_external_queue.py`
Discord ingestion-side IO is ledgered: `tests/test_discord_ingestion_ledger.py` asserts pending rows are inserted before each Storage/Groq call and transitioned to confirmed/failed afterwards

All Sprint 1a tests pass UNCHANGED (`tests/test_envelope.py`, `tests/test_ledger.py`, `tests/test_run_turn.py`, `tests/test_cli.py`, `tests/test_tool_kit.py`, the existing assertions in `tests/test_sqlite_store.py`); only additive new test cases are added
No literal Supabase service-role JWT, Discord bot token, Anthropic key, or Groq key appears in any committed file under the repo (migrations, tests, fixtures, docs)
The existing `Transport` Protocol in `agent_kit/ports.py` is unchanged. `PushTransport` is added as a separate Protocol

`agent_kit/loop.py` changes are limited to the optional kwargs (`triggered_by_message_ids`, `recovered_input_messages`, `on_turn_start`, `mid_turn_message_check`), the `idempotency_key` thread, the `request_body` recording, the explicit-send_message gating block, the `system_seq` field add to `request_summary`, and the vision-block detection in tool-result construction
Each new module is under ~400 lines (`agent_kit/store/supabase.py`, `agent_kit/resident.py`, `agent_kit/transport/discord.py`)

A deferral note is recorded for invocation-mode attachments (`--attach`, `attachments=`, `LocalBlobStore`) and the `transcribe_voice` tool / non-voice-audio path, so future planners can find the open debt

Manual smoke against staging Discord + staging Supabase: whitelisted DM replies within 30s; voice DM transcribes; image attachment lands in Storage with `images` row; mid-turn DM updates the status message in the same turn

assumptions: Discord whitelist via env var `DISCORD_USER_WHITELIST` (SD-001); restart required to change.
Supabase store uses direct psycopg3 (SD-002); supabase-py is used only for Storage.
Supabase epic lock is row-based on `epic_locks` table (SD-003).
`images` table lives in BOTH SQLite and Supabase migration trees this sprint (SD-004).

Mid-turn handling implements the spec's same-turn end-of-turn re-prompt (SD-005). The hook fires both at final-text time AND before each explicit `send_message` tool invocation, so resident-mode `send_message` cannot bypass the check.
`tests/store_contract.py` stays UNCHANGED from Sprint 1a (SD-006); new methods are tested in `tests/store_contract_v1b.py`.
`PushTransport` is a new Protocol (SD-007) added alongside the existing `Transport`; CLI continues to bypass `Transport`.
Image and voice work this sprint is Discord-only (SD-008). Invocation-mode attachments and `transcribe_voice` are deferred.
ToolContext gains optional `transport`, `blob`, `external_queue` fields (SD-009). Default `None` preserves invocation-mode shape.

External-request ledger gets a new nullable `request_body` JSONB column (in BOTH stores' migration trees) so Anthropic/OpenAI replay has durable material. The existing `request_summary` keeps its small-shape diagnostic role.

The audit wrapper preserves Sprint 1a atomicity: tool body runs inside the transaction; mutation+tool_calls commit together. Only post-commit network callables are queued. Existing rollback test at `tests/test_tool_kit.py:27` is unchanged.

Discord ingestion-side IO (Storage upload, Groq transcription) is ledgered through `external_requests` with `tool_call_id=null`, `turn_id=null`, and an idempotency key derived from the inbound `discord_message_id`. Reconciler dispatches them like any other pending row.
`Blob.exists(ref) -> bool` is a small additive method on the existing Blob Protocol; `SupabaseStorageBlob` implements it via storage HEAD.
`PushTransport.fetch_recent_messages(channel_id, since, until)` is part of the new Protocol from day one (not added later).
Reconciler runs at startup AND every 5 minutes in resident mode; invocation/CLI does not run the periodic timer.
GitHub provider reconciliation is a no-op log this sprint.
The Supabase service-role JWT supplied in the idea block must be rotated by the user.
